replies.msg:
各位大英帝國做緊嘢／將會做嘢嘅巴絲打，歡迎入嚟呢到鳩噏扮下工

Q&A credit to 滑蛋豬仔飯

Q: 如果無BC又想係英國打工係咪好難?

A:
通常會比working visa一係大公司
D細公司仔都見過有批不過當然會少D

老老實實渣BNO/特區係就係難D不過都唔係無可能
都識得有人搞得掂個visa
以前有同學會係cv度寫明話要working visa
如果間公司都肯in你既話多數即係肯批
唔洗浪費大家時間
大小公司都有 試下無妨

再唔係學其他巴打講
申請左working holiday做住先都未嘗不可
當然之後整唔整到tier 2 working visa就唔敢講
不過起碼都有2年比你係度做下野儲下經驗先

如果做開比較專業行業既朋友其實拎左working holiday visa先，再轉tier 2既機會都幾樂觀，都識幾個朋友都係咁，好似都係做design / I.T.之類 (by 賽麗娜Serena)


Q: 想問下英國見工係咪同香港一樣都係上網報?

A:
如果你話搵工過程其實同香港差唔多
睇你咩工啦
普通黎講大公司既話都係cv cover letter -> online test -> phone in-> assessment centre ( 可以好多round) -> final in
細公司就少幾個步驟咁


Q: 想問下係咪bno 同特區都可以fg 一年tier5 先，唔洗特登要搵肯比tier2 既公司住

A:
你英國畢業定香港想過去？ 唔係英國畢業唔肯定當地申請有無唔同但BNO 隨時可以申請tier 5 重有兩年，特區要抽架，都係兩年。
當地申請可能短d , 我當初因爲公司未令到可以簽visa 既licence 就幫我申請住tier 5 先，得特區又過左抽籤所以係visa 公司幫我係英國申請，但咁就得一年.. 無深究點解。
但全部香港申請過去既都有兩年。 (by 花生哥)

Q: 下年打算去working holiday 會唔會好難搵工同搵地方住（想住反係zoom1,2) 聽人講就話最好係香港搵定先去係好定唔好 我就做開酒吧個邊會唔會香港人 真心求教

A:
英國bar tender既需求量其實好大 因為佢pub既數量基本上同香港7仔有得揮 甚至係正常既餐廳入面都會有間pub仔/bar比人淨飲野吹水
所以你有經驗根本唔洗驚 去到英國任何一個地方都一定搵到野做

講我自己經驗啦 我就做好typical既西餐廳既 入面全部鬼佬
因為要升supervisor 所以gm要求我識哂餐廳所有部份 包括floor hatch同bar
Bar嗰邊我完全係一張白紙 連用咖啡機都唔識 基本上淨係識開水喉倒果汁呢d白痴都識做既野
去到依家講緊一個在既時間 叫做忙起上黎可以打哂三邊 聽日冇人返比人編左去自己睇bar

我覺得做bar最緊要係識同客吹水 尤其係餐廳bar
好似我地咁 係住宅區 長期都有熟客日日黎打"等" 真係日日風雨不改黎報到
黎到冇野做咪同bar tender吹水囉 所以你一定要好吹得

其餘都冇乜野擔心 驚咪去indeed search下囉 一大炸請緊人 (by 小野屋杏)

Q: 英國黎緊暑假畢業，準備搵工 （宜家4月中係咪太遲)

A: 唔遲
雖然有啲係截左
但仲有好多
留學生有優勢 就係唔怕relocation
成個英國既工你都apply得
宜家真係開始send cv都唔會太遲既
好多大公司如果唔係grad scheme既話其實一路都請緊人
以前好多同學都係8/9月先有offer
有D直頭下年1月先有工開
所以唔洗擔心既肯踏出第一步搵工就ok (by 馬壯FN2)

Do you know that if you have British National (Overseas) status, you can get British Citizenship via registration using form B(OTA) which is cheaper than naturalisation.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

2017 fees - Registration £1,163 vs Naturalisation £1,282.

https://www.gov.uk/government/uploads/system/uploads/attachment_data/file/607005/NationalityFeesLeaflet_2017.pdf

You still need ILR


Once you get ILR for a year, then you can use form B(OTA) to apply for British Citizenship.

Don't go for naturalisation if you have BN(O) status - registration will give you the same thing and it is cheaper!

Do you know that if you have British National (Overseas) status, you can get British Citizenship via registration using form B(OTA) which is cheaper than naturalisation.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

2017 fees - Registration £1,163 vs Naturalisation £1,282.

https://www.gov.uk/government/uploads/system/uploads/attachment_data/file/607005/NationalityFeesLeaflet_2017.pdf

You still need ILR


Once you get ILR for a year, then you can use form B(OTA) to apply for British Citizenship.

Don't go for naturalisation if you have BN(O) status - registration will give you the same thing and it is cheaper!

重點bota份form短d

Bno早兩個月過左期

Do you know that if you have British National (Overseas) status, you can get British Citizenship via registration using form B(OTA) which is cheaper than naturalisation.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

2017 fees - Registration £1,163 vs Naturalisation £1,282.

https://www.gov.uk/government/uploads/system/uploads/attachment_data/file/607005/NationalityFeesLeaflet_2017.pdf

You still need ILR


Once you get ILR for a year, then you can use form B(OTA) to apply for British Citizenship.

重點bota份form短d

You can go to the local Post Office to get a passport renewal form (the same form for all British passport application - including BN(O)). All you need to do is to send your expired BN(O) passport, with your HKID card and the completed form with payment back to the Passport Office.

There are also passport customer service centres around the country for urgent cases. There is one in Newport.

https://www.gov.uk/get-a-passport-urgently

If you are willing to pay up (£128), you can have your passport in four hours.

1 week counter service - £103

Normal service by post - £72.50

Those with British National (Overseas) status living in the UK for more than 6 months can register to vote in all UK elections.

You will need to register to vote by Monday 22 May 2017 in order to vote in the general election on 8 June 2017.

https://www.yourvotematters.co.uk/uk-general-election-2017

If you are too busy and cannot visit the polling station on election day, you can apply to vote by post. In order to vote by post in the general election on 8 June 2017, you need to apply for a postal vote by the 23 May 2017.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/apply-for-a-postal-vote

If you want to get your ILR ASAP and your case is not complex, you can pay extra to get same day processing at a Visa Premium Service Centre. You will need to pay an extra £590 on top of your application fee to use the Visa Premium Service Centre. Add another £75 for Saturday and after office hours (9am to 4pm) appointments.

So total fees will be -

£2,297 (ILR fees)+£590 (Premium Service)=£2,887 (using 2017 fees)

https://www.gov.uk/ukvi-premium-service-centres

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/visa-regulations-revised-table

There is a Visa Premium Service Centre in Cardiff.

You need to remember, the quicker you get ILR, the faster you can become a British Citizen.

If you want to get your ILR ASAP and your case is not complex, you can pay extra to get same day processing at a Visa Premium Service Centre. You will need to pay an extra £590 on top of your application fee to use the Visa Premium Service Centre. Add another £75 for Saturday and after office hours (9am to 4pm) appointments.

So total fees will be -

£2,297 (ILR fees)+£590 (Premium Service)=£2,887 (using 2017 fees)

https://www.gov.uk/ukvi-premium-service-centres

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/visa-regulations-revised-table

There is a Visa Premium Service Centre in Cardiff.

You need to remember, the quicker you get ILR, the faster you can become a British Citizen.

so expensive...


You also need to add another £1,163 for registering to become a British Citizen (British National (Overseas) passport holders using B(OTA) form) or £1,282 for naturalisation (HKSAR passport holders). (Both figures based on 2017 fees)

It is best to apply British Citizenship as soon as possible after getting Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR), as ILR status will be revoked if the holder left the UK for two years or more.

If you want to get your ILR ASAP and your case is not complex, you can pay extra to get same day processing at a Visa Premium Service Centre. You will need to pay an extra £590 on top of your application fee to use the Visa Premium Service Centre. Add another £75 for Saturday and after office hours (9am to 4pm) appointments.

So total fees will be -

£2,297 (ILR fees)+£590 (Premium Service)=£2,887 (using 2017 fees)

https://www.gov.uk/ukvi-premium-service-centres

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/visa-regulations-revised-table

There is a Visa Premium Service Centre in Cardiff.

You need to remember, the quicker you get ILR, the faster you can become a British Citizen.

so expensive...


You also need to add another £1,163 for registering to become a British Citizen (British National (Overseas) passport holders using B(OTA) form) or £1,282 for naturalisation (HKSAR passport holders). (Both figures based on 2017 fees)

It is best to apply British Citizenship as soon as possible after getting Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR), as ILR status will be revoked if the holder left the UK for two years or more.

Q: 想問下係咪bno 同特區都可以fg 一年tier5 先，唔洗特登要搵肯比tier2 既公司住

A:
你英國畢業定香港想過去？ 唔係英國畢業唔肯定當地申請有無唔同但BNO 隨時可以申請tier 5 重有兩年，特區要抽架，都係兩年。
當地申請可能短d , 我當初因爲公司未令到可以簽visa 既licence 就幫我申請住tier 5 先，得特區又過左抽籤所以係visa 公司幫我係英國申請，但咁就得一年.. 無深究點解。
但全部香港申請過去既都有兩年。 (by 花生哥)


我想問下係咪英國畢業既可以直接轉TIER5唔洗先返香港? (拎BNO)

我想問下係咪英國畢業既可以直接轉TIER5唔洗先返香港? (拎BNO)

可以Tier 4 Switch to Tier 5 (Temporary Work)
Switch 既定義就係apply from within the UK


不過你Visa 應該十月先到期？仲有成五個月唔好咁早打定輸數
努力搵下Tier 2 sponsorship 先啦


Tier 5有兩種
一種係Temporary Worker
另一種先係Youth Mobility

如果你想直接switch to Tier 5, 你同公司傾下Temporary Work 果個方向先 依個就得一年
因為依種方法你公司都有些少手續要搞但就唔洗licensing

至於Youth Mobility 就好似無得Switch
要返香港Apply

居英europian 唔投得票?
有個揸德國passport既話唔投得


Only the follow people can vote in UK general elections (House of Commons elections) -

1) British Citizen
2) Irish Citizen
3) Commonwealth Citizen (including British National (Overseas) passport holders) with greater than six months leave in the UK (for example student visa holders, working holiday permit holders or spouse visa holders).

European citizens living in the UK cannot vote in general elections (class as 'national' elections). They can only vote in local and European Parliament elections.

Yes indeed. Please see link below -

http://www.electoralcommission.org.uk/__data/assets/pdf_file/0007/43954/Part-B-Entitlement-to-register-March-2010.pdf

Go to Part B Page 19 - there is a section on Hong Kong.

Hong Kong
6.23 Following its transfer to Chinese sovereignty on 1 July 1997, Hong Kong
was deleted from the list of British Overseas Territories. As a result, former
residents of Hong Kong are not qualifying Commonwealth citizens by virtue of
their residency in Hong Kong as Hong Kong Chinese is no longer a
nationality.
6.24 If an elector declares their nationality to be Hong Kong Chinese then the
Electoral Registration Officer should exercise their powers to require evidence
of the elector’s actual nationality and confirm the type of passport that they
hold.
6.25 Any previous resident of Hong Kong who holds a British Dependent
Territories, British Nationals (Overseas) or British Overseas passport meets
the nationality criteria for all elections in the UK.
6.26 Any previous resident of Hong Kong who only has a Chinese Special
Administrative Region passport is Chinese and may not register.


As long as the BN(O) passport holder has leave to remain in the UK for greater than six months (for example student visa or working holiday permit), then he/she can register to vote in all UK elections.

If you want to vote in next month's general election, you need to register to vote by the 22 May 2017.

Only the follow people can vote in UK general elections (House of Commons elections) -

1) British Citizen
2) Irish Citizen
3) Commonwealth Citizen (including British National (Overseas) passport holders) with greater than six months leave in the UK (for example student visa holders, working holiday permit holders or spouse visa holders).

European citizens living in the UK cannot vote in general elections (class as 'national' elections). They can only vote in local and European Parliament elections.
